subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for Conventional Commit checkpoint subjects

sources.label: Conventional Commits 1.0.0 spec
sources.summary: Format <type>[optional scope]: <description>. Types beyond feat/fix (e.g. chore) allowed (item 14) and have no implicit SemVer effect unless BREAKING CHANGE (FAQ/SemVer). Footer tokens follow git-trailer convention (items 6-9); token MUST use '-' for whitespace except BREAKING CHANGE.

sources.label: Live spec law rq-cc03.1 (advance-delivery/spec.json L454-467)
sources.summary: Current law mandates subject 'task({taskId}): {mode}' and body Change/Task/Mode/Verification with multiple -m args. KD1/KD4 must overwrite the subject clause; body fields already match objective 2.

sources.label: Current implementation buildCommitMessage (checkpoint.ts)
sources.summary: Emits subject 'task(${taskId}): cancel â€” ${reason}' / 'task(${taskId}): completed' with SUBJECT_MAX_LEN=72, CANCEL_REASON_MAX_LEN=64; truncates reason into subject. Body fields Change/Task/Mode/Verification already present. Confirms the bug and the truncation pattern KD2/KD3 target.

sources.label: adv_spec show advance-delivery parse failure
sources.summary: Tool returned Zod parse error: requirements[18-22].meta.merged_from expected string received undefined. Confirms AC9/KD4 spec parse drift is live and blocks adv_spec show validation.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is correct, minimal, and spec-aware. KD1 (fixed chore(adv) prefix) is directly supported by Conventional Commits 1.0.0: chore is a valid non-feat/fix type with no SemVer effect absent BREAKING CHANGE, satisfying objective 4 (release-neutral) without repo-policy heuristics (DDC4/avoidance respected). KD2 (Reason in body) aligns with the git-trailer footer convention and removes the current subject truncation (verified in buildCommitMessage). Body audit fields already exist in the implementation, so objective 2 is preserved with no metadata loss. KD4 spec drift is real: adv_spec show advance-delivery currently fails to parse due to missing meta.merged_from strings on requirements[18-22], so AC9 repair is genuinely required, not speculative. rq-cc03.1's existing subject clause ('task({taskId}): {mode}') directly contradicts the new contract and MUST be rewritten â€” this is the one hard spec touch, correctly scoped in KD4.

validation.notes: No conflicts. Two cautions: (1) verify 'Reason:' stays a single-word footer token (CC spec item 9 requires '-' for whitespace in tokens; 'Reason' is fine, but if any multi-word body label is added it must hyphenate). (2) KD3/DDC3 length guard: with fixed prefix 'chore(adv): checkpoint ' (~23 chars) + task ID, normal ADV task IDs (tk-XXXXXXXX, ~11 chars) yield ~34-char subjects, far under 72 â€” so deterministic rejection is effectively a guard for pathological/overlength IDs only; ensure the guard rejects rather than truncates to satisfy AC3 and avoid misleading audit refs.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED â€” proceed with the design as written. All four KDs are evidence-backed: chore(adv) is canonical and release-neutral per Conventional Commits 1.0.0, the Reason-in-body move follows the git-trailer footer convention, and the rq-cc03.1 subject rewrite plus the meta.merged_from parse-repair (AC9) are both confirmed necessary against the live spec. No materially simpler alternative exists that still satisfies objective 1 (CC-compatible subjects) â€” keeping the old 'task(...)' form is the only simpler path and it is precisely the bug. Minor follow-ups: keep body footer tokens single-word/hyphenated per CC item 9, and ensure KD3 rejects overlength subjects deterministically rather than truncating task IDs (AC3/DDC3). Bound the spec edit to the rq-cc03.1 subject clause and the malformed meta.merged_from fields only, per the design's stated scope.
follow_ups: Confirm KD3 guard path rejects (not truncates) overlength subject inputs to satisfy AC3/DDC3.
When rewriting rq-cc03.1, update both complete and cancel subject regex examples and ensure no other advance-delivery scenario references the old 'task({taskId})' subject form (AC8).

documents.proposal:
## Cross-Project Origin

This change was created as a follow-up from **sharperflow-security-gates**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Source project | sharperflow-security-gates |
| Source path | `/home/jon/dev/sharperflow-security-gates` |

> **Note:** The originating project should be consulted during discovery for the Conventional Commit gate failure context.

# Proposal: Fix checkpoint commits

## Why
ADV checkpoint commits use `task(...)` subjects that can fail Conventional Commit PR gates. This forces agents/users to squash or rewrite branch history after normal ADV apply work, even though checkpointing is required by ADV workflow policy.

## What Changes
- Make ADV checkpoint commit subjects compatible with standard Conventional Commit gates.
- Preserve task/change audit metadata in durable checkpoint commit bodies and task completion state.
- Update ADV docs and tests so documented checkpoint commit examples match generated output.

## User Outcomes
- ADV users can complete task checkpointing without repository commit-policy failures caused by ADV-generated subjects.
- Repository owners keep Conventional Commit enforcement without custom exceptions for ADV checkpoints.
- Reviewers can still trace checkpoint commits to the ADV change, task, mode, verification summary, and cancel reason when present.

## Affected Code
- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` â€” checkpoint commit message generation and related inline documentation.
- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.test.ts` or adjacent checkpoint tests â€” subject/body assertions.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` â€” generated checkpoint commit examples.
- `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md` and `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json` â€” checkpoint commit subject law.
- Any other repo docs found during discovery that cite `task(tk-xxxx): ...` checkpoint subjects.

## Related Repositories
- Current repo: `advance`.
- Originating repo: `sharperflow-security-gates`, which surfaced the release friction.
- No code changes expected in the originating repo for this change.

## Constraints
- Preserve checkpoint commits; do not remove or bypass checkpointing.
- Preserve task completion/cancellation semantics and Temporal signal behavior.
- Keep audit metadata machine-readable in commit body text.
- Avoid repo-specific commit-policy detection or allowlist heuristics.
- Keep generated subject deterministic for valid ADV task IDs.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Change ADV checkpoint commit subject generation.
- Keep checkpoint commit body metadata for change ID, task ID, mode, verification summary, and cancel reason.
- Update local tests, docs, and specs that describe generated checkpoint subjects.
- Verify relevant checkpoint tests and repo smoke/full test path as appropriate.

### Out of Scope
- Changing task lifecycle semantics.
- Changing git checkpoint timing or when checkpoints are required.
- Rewriting existing branch history or existing checkpoint commits.
- Adding repo-specific Conventional Commit policy integration.
- Modifying `sharperflow-security-gates`.

### Must Not
- Do not drop checkpoint audit metadata.
- Do not require agents to manually squash or rewrite ADV checkpoint commits.
- Do not make correctness depend on heuristic detection of repository commit policy.

## Impact
- Reduces release friction for ADV-managed changes in Conventional Commit-gated repositories.
- Keeps ADV checkpoint behavior deterministic and auditable.
- Requires test/doc/spec updates near checkpoint commit generation.

## Context
Verified quick checks:
- `buildCommitMessage` exists at `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` and currently emits `task(${taskId}): completed` or `task(${taskId}): cancel â€” <reason>`.
- Current `task(tk-...)` examples appear in `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`.
- Checkpoint commit law exists in `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md` under `rq-cc03.1`: `task({taskId}): {mode}`.
- `adv_spec show advance-delivery` currently fails because several `meta.merged_from` fields are malformed; implementation must still update the tracked spec artifact and may need to repair parse drift.
- Conventional Commits 1.0.0 uses `<type>[optional scope]: <description>`; `chore(adv): checkpoint tk-abc` fits that structure.

## Discovery Agenda
- Confirm exact current `buildCommitMessage` behavior and existing test coverage.
- Confirm valid ADV task ID length/shape and choose structural handling for overlength subjects.
- Confirm all docs/tests/specs that cite checkpoint subject format.
- Confirm verification scope: targeted checkpoint tests plus repo smoke/check path needed before acceptance.

## Ambiguity Scan
- Boundaries: clear. Scope includes in-scope, out-of-scope, and must-not items.
- Functional scope: clear. User outcomes state desired externally observable effects without engineering acceptance criteria.
- Completion signals: clear enough for discovery; engineering AC belong in discovery/agreement.

# Discovery Findings

## Discovery Checklist

| Step | Status | Result |
|---|---:|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Loaded `adv-agent-tool-contracts` and `adv-ci-release`; both relevant. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md` found but not directly applicable; no checkpoint-specific prior research pack found. |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS | No overlapping active change found. Own-change warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` expected pre-prep. Agenda has many unrelated follow-ups; no checkpoint-subject blocker found. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases listed below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Open questions annotated below. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | `rq-cc03.1` needs update; possible parse-drift repair for `advance-delivery` spec. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Same-pattern references found in code, docs, and spec mirror. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Standard Conventional Commit header is best fit; external-solution check skipped because no external tool/library alternative is viable. |

## Skills Considered

| Skill | Match | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `adv-agent-tool-contracts` | Relevant: agent-callable `adv_task_checkpoint` behavior and tests | Loaded; apply schema/tool/test/spec checklist. |
| `adv-ci-release` | Relevant: Conventional Commit gates and release-neutral checkpoint commits | Loaded; prefer conventional subject that does not depend on repo exceptions. |
| `adv-clarify` | Not needed yet | Discovery questions are concrete and bounded. |

## Extends

- No checkpoint-specific prior research found.
- `docs/adv-run-test-prep.md` is adjacent only; new finding: this change should use repo-local verification routing and avoid broad `adv_run_test` scope.

## Conflict Scan

- Active changes: no direct checkpoint commit subject overlap found in active ADV changes.
- Validation: passed with expected pre-prep warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS`.
- Agenda: no direct checkpoint subject item identified in visible agenda slice.
- Lineage: source path `/home/jon/dev/sharperflow-security-gates` exists and matches source project name; user must confirm origin context before agreement persistence.

## Current State

- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` generates commit messages in `buildCommitMessage`.
- Complete subject today: `task(${taskId}): completed` truncated to 72 chars.
- Cancel subject today: `task(${taskId}): cancel â€” <reason>`, with reason truncated by subject budget.
- Commit body today includes `Change`, `Task`, `Mode`, and `Verification` when present.
- Cancel reason currently lives in subject, not body.
- Checkpoint tests exercise signal/clean/dirty/error behavior but do not directly assert Conventional Commit subject/body format.
- Spec mirror `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md` still requires `task({taskId}): {mode}`.

## Edge Cases

1. Overlength task ID or unexpected task ID shape.
   - Risk: subject exceeds 72 chars or malformed conventional header.
   - Handling target: deterministic validation/failure or bounded subject construction; no partial malformed header.
2. Long cancel reason.
   - Risk: previous reason-in-subject truncation hid full reason.
   - Handling target: subject excludes reason; body includes `Reason: <reason>` for cancel.
3. Missing `changeId`.
   - Risk: body lacks `Change` if derivation fails.
   - Handling target: preserve current optional behavior unless existing guards require change ID.
4. Clean tree checkpoint.
   - Risk: no commit created, so subject logic not exercised.
   - Handling target: behavior unchanged; tests cover dirty tree commit path for subject/body.

## Open Design Questions

| Question | Trust model | Blast radius | Alternatives | Recommended answer |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Should checkpoint subjects be release-neutral? | Joint: release policy affects users | Wrong answer can suppress or trigger auto-release behavior | `chore(adv)` vs `fix(adv)`/`docs(adv)` | `chore(adv)` so checkpoint commits satisfy gates without causing semver release. |
| Should cancel reason move to body? | Agent-resolved | Wrong answer can leak long/freeform text into subject | Keep reason in subject vs body | Move to body as `Reason: ...`; subject remains deterministic. |
| How handle `advance-delivery` spec parse drift? | Agent-resolved, scope-visible | Ignoring law drift can leave `adv_spec` broken | Repair alongside delta vs agenda follow-up | Repair if required to update spec cleanly; keep focused to malformed `meta.merged_from`. |
| Is origin context expected? | User-only | Wrong context can solve wrong repo pain | Confirm origin vs detach from origin | Ask user before agreement. |

## Draft Spec Deltas

### `rq-cc03.1` â€” Structured commit message subject compatibility

Given:
- A dirty-tree checkpoint succeeds for `complete` or `cancel` mode.

When:
- The commit is created.

Then:
- The commit subject MUST be a Conventional Commit header with `chore(adv)` type/scope.
- Complete mode subject MUST match `chore(adv): checkpoint {taskId}` for valid ADV task IDs.
- Cancel mode subject MUST match `chore(adv): cancel checkpoint {taskId}` for valid ADV task IDs.
- The body MUST include `Change: {changeId}` when available.
- The body MUST include `Task: {taskId}`.
- The body MUST include `Mode: {mode}`.
- The body MUST include `Reason: {reason}` for cancel mode when a reason is supplied.
- The body MUST include `Verification: {verification}` when verification is supplied.
- The message MUST be created with multiple `-m` args for subject + body/trailers.

## Related Pattern Scan

Same-pattern references found:
- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` docstring and `buildCommitMessage` implementation.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` checkpoint subject documentation.
- `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md` and `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json` requirement `rq-cc03.1`.
- `README.md` discusses checkpoint commits but does not cite the bad subject form.

## LBP Check

- Best fit: fixed Conventional Commit-compatible header `chore(adv): ...` plus structured body metadata.
- Why: Conventional Commits 1.0.0 supports `<type>[optional scope]: <description>` and optional body. `chore` is a recognized release-neutral type in common auto-release setups.
- Rejected: repo-specific allowlist detection; it makes correctness heuristic and still leaks policy into ADV checkpointing.
- External-solution check: skipped; no external tool/library choice is involved.

## Recommended Objectives

1. Emit Conventional Commit-compatible checkpoint subjects for complete and cancel modes.
2. Preserve machine-readable audit body fields for change ID, task ID, mode, verification, and cancel reason.
3. Update spec/docs/tests so the generated subject contract is enforced structurally.
4. Keep checkpoint commits local/auditable and release-neutral by default.

## AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:P

| ID | Severity | Category | Evidence | Resolution |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | HIGH | Missing Information | `This change was created as a follow-up from **sharperflow-security-gates**.` | Ask user to confirm origin context. Single HIGH; continue to clarification round. |
documents.problemStatement: ADV checkpoint commits use `task(...)` subjects that can fail Conventional Commit PR gates. This forces agents/users to squash or rewrite branch history after normal ADV apply work, even though checkpointing is required by ADV workflow policy.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Fix checkpoint commits

## Objectives

1. Emit Conventional Commit-compatible checkpoint subjects for complete and cancel modes.
2. Preserve machine-readable audit body fields for change ID, task ID, mode, verification, and cancel reason.
3. Update spec/docs/tests so generated checkpoint subject contract is enforced structurally.
4. Keep checkpoint commits local/auditable and release-neutral by default.

## Success Criteria

- ADV-generated checkpoint commits pass standard Conventional Commit header checks without repo-specific exceptions.
- Checkpoint commits remain release-neutral by default: `chore(adv)` subject, no semver bump intent.
- Audit trace remains visible in commit body: change ID when available, task ID, mode, verification, and cancel reason when present.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Complete checkpoint dirty-tree commit subject matches `^chore\(adv\): checkpoint tk-[A-Za-z0-9]+$`.
- AC2: Cancel checkpoint dirty-tree commit subject matches `^chore\(adv\): cancel checkpoint tk-[A-Za-z0-9]+$`.
- AC3: Complete and cancel checkpoint subjects are â‰¤72 chars for valid ADV task IDs, or invalid/overlength input fails deterministically before malformed commit output.
- AC4: Commit body includes `Task: <task-id>` and `Mode: complete|cancel`.
- AC5: Commit body includes `Change: <change-id>` when available.
- AC6: Commit body includes `Verification: <summary>` when verification supplied.
- AC7: Cancel commit body includes `Reason: <reason>` when reason supplied; cancel reason is not embedded in subject.
- AC8: ADV checkpoint docs/specs no longer require or cite `task(tk-xxxx): ...` generated subjects.
- AC9: If `advance-delivery` spec parse drift blocks spec update, malformed metadata is repaired only enough to make `adv_spec show advance-delivery` parse.
- AC10: Targeted checkpoint tests pass, and repo smoke/check path reports success.

## Constraints

- Preserve checkpoint commits; do not remove or bypass checkpointing.
- Preserve task completion/cancellation semantics and Temporal signal behavior.
- Keep audit metadata machine-readable in commit body text.
- Avoid repo-specific commit-policy detection or allowlist heuristics.
- Keep generated subject deterministic for valid ADV task IDs.

## Avoidances

- Do not drop checkpoint audit metadata.
- Do not require agents to manually squash or rewrite ADV checkpoint commits.
- Do not make correctness depend on heuristic detection of repository commit policy.
- Do not modify `sharperflow-security-gates` in this change.
- Do not rewrite existing branch history or existing checkpoint commits.

## Out of Scope

- Changing task lifecycle semantics.
- Changing git checkpoint timing or when checkpoints are required.
- Adding repo-specific Conventional Commit policy integration.
- Modifying the originating repository.

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false`

Rationale: change affects TypeScript tool behavior, specs, tests, and documentation. No browser-visible UI or visual output surface.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- Origin context: use `sharperflow-security-gates` as source context for the checkpoint-commit friction; make no changes there.
- Release behavior: no preference; ADV chooses release-neutral behavior from evidence.
- Spec repair: repair malformed `advance-delivery` spec metadata only if needed to update checkpoint subject law.
- Criteria: user approved the listed success and acceptance criteria.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Use `chore(adv)` as fixed Conventional Commit type/scope. Conventional Commits 1.0.0 supports `<type>[optional scope]: <description>`, and `chore` is common release-neutral commit type.
- Move cancel reason out of subject into body as `Reason: ...` to keep header deterministic and â‰¤72 chars.
- Update checkpoint subject law in `advance-delivery` spec because existing `rq-cc03.1` currently requires the old `task({taskId}): {mode}` subject.
- Keep clean-tree checkpoint behavior unchanged because no commit subject is created for clean tree.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria approved by user in chat: `approve`.
documents.design:
# Design: Fix checkpoint commits

## Architecture Overview

`adv_task_checkpoint` keeps its current lifecycle: validate repo/branch/HEAD, detect clean vs dirty tree, stage dirty changes, create a local checkpoint commit, then record completion/cancellation state through existing Temporal signals. The change is limited to deterministic commit-message construction and the laws/docs/tests that describe it.

Core mechanism:

- Generate release-neutral Conventional Commit subjects in `buildCommitMessage`.
- Keep audit metadata in commit body fields.
- Add structural tests around subject/body output and git commit arguments.
- Update `advance-delivery` checkpoint law and docs so old `task(...)` subjects disappear.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 â€” Fixed `chore(adv)` subject prefix

Use fixed type/scope `chore(adv)` for all checkpoint commit subjects.

- Complete: `chore(adv): checkpoint <task-id>`
- Cancel: `chore(adv): cancel checkpoint <task-id>`

Rationale:

- Conventional Commits 1.0.0 supports `<type>[optional scope]: <description>`.
- `chore` is release-neutral in common release workflows.
- Fixed prefix avoids repo-specific policy detection and keeps correctness deterministic.

### KD2 â€” Cancel reason in body, not subject

Move cancel reason from subject to body as `Reason: <reason>` when supplied.

Rationale:

- Keeps subject short and stable.
- Preserves full audit context better than subject truncation.
- Reduces freeform text in commit header.

### KD3 â€” Reject malformed or overlength subject inputs before commit

Keep normal ADV task IDs in the subject. Add a structural guard so malformed or overlength task IDs fail before `git commit` rather than truncating task IDs.

Rationale:

- AC3 allows deterministic rejection for overlength input.
- Rejection preserves audit correctness better than silently truncating a task ID into a misleading reference.

### KD4 â€” Update checkpoint law and repair parse drift only if needed

Update `rq-cc03.1` in `advance-delivery` to require Conventional Commit subjects and body `Reason` for cancel. Repair only malformed `meta.merged_from` fields needed for `adv_spec show advance-delivery` to parse.

Rationale:

- Existing spec law currently requires old subject form.
- Validator confirmed `adv_spec show advance-delivery` currently fails on malformed `requirements[18-22].meta.merged_from`, so AC9 is live.
- Repair scope stays bounded to making the targeted spec law readable and valid.

## ADR Drafts

None. Decisions are local, low-reversal-cost, and directly explained by tests/spec law.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **RED tests**
   - Add focused `buildCommitMessage` tests for complete and cancel subjects.
   - Assert subject regexes and â‰¤72-character budget.
   - Assert body fields: `Change`, `Task`, `Mode`, `Verification`, `Reason`.
   - Add or adjust commit-path test to assert `git commit -m <subject> -m <body>` receives new subject/body.
   - Add deterministic rejection test for task ID input that would exceed subject budget.

2. **Message generation**
   - Change `buildCommitMessage` subject construction to `chore(adv): checkpoint ${taskId}` and `chore(adv): cancel checkpoint ${taskId}`.
   - Add deterministic guard for malformed/overlength task IDs before commit message use.
   - Preserve existing optional `Change` and `Verification` body behavior.
   - Add `Reason` body line for cancel when `reason` is non-empty.

3. **Spec/docs update**
   - Update `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md` and `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json` for `rq-cc03.1`.
   - Repair malformed `meta.merged_from` fields in `advance-delivery` only enough for `adv_spec show advance-delivery` to parse.
   - Update `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` and `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts` comments.
   - Scan for remaining generated-subject references to `task(tk-xxxx): ...`.

4. **Verification**
   - Run targeted checkpoint tests.
   - Run `adv_spec show advance-delivery` after spec update.
   - Run repo smoke/check path for final confidence.

## LBP Analysis

Chosen approach is the long-term best practice because the commit-message contract is a deterministic tool function with tests and spec law. It avoids policy guessing, avoids repository-specific allowlists, and uses a standard Conventional Commit header while preserving ADV-specific audit data in the body.

Rejected alternatives:

- **Keep `task(...)` and ask agents to squash** â€” preserves friction and makes checkpoint behavior leak into PR policy.
- **Detect repo commit policy** â€” heuristic, brittle, and violates structural correctness.
- **Use `fix(adv)`** â€” passes many gates but can incorrectly trigger release automation if checkpoint commits reach trunk.
- **Remove checkpoint commits** â€” violates checkpoint/audit objectives and existing ADV workflow law.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.ts`
  - `buildCommitMessage`
  - inline checkpoint subject docs
  - pre-commit subject validation helper
- `plugin/src/tools/checkpoint.test.ts`
  - direct message tests
  - dirty-tree commit argument tests
  - deterministic rejection test
- `docs/specs/advance-delivery.md`
  - `rq-cc03.1` structured commit message scenario
- `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json`
  - mirrored law and bounded `meta.merged_from` repair
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`
  - Task Checkpoint Commits subject examples

## Design-Derived Criteria

- DDC1: `buildCommitMessage` remains a pure deterministic function with direct unit coverage.
- DDC2: `git commit` still uses multiple `-m` args: one subject and one body.
- DDC3: Subject construction must not truncate task IDs into misleading audit references; reject invalid/overlength subject inputs instead.
- DDC4: No runtime inspection of repository commit-policy config is introduced.
- DDC5: Clean-tree checkpoint behavior remains unchanged because no commit is created.
- DDC6: `advance-delivery` spec repair is limited to malformed metadata needed for parse success and the `rq-cc03.1` subject/body law update.

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Spec JSON parse drift expands scope | Repair only malformed fields blocking `advance-delivery`; add no unrelated spec changes. |
| Existing tests only assert signal behavior, not commit message | Add direct `buildCommitMessage` and git commit argument assertions. |
| Long task IDs exceed subject budget | Use pre-commit deterministic rejection or validation, not silent truncation. |
| Checkpoint commits could still be pushed by humans | Existing checkpoint publication boundary remains unchanged; this change does not grant push/archive authority. |
| Release tooling differs by repo | Use broadly valid Conventional Commit format and release-neutral `chore`; no repo-specific detection. |

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout: skipped â€” narrow deterministic tool-contract change. Opportunity surface is limited to replacing one commit-message function and aligned docs/spec/tests. No external implementation alternatives or user-value tradeoffs remain after discovery.

## Validator Result

Verdict: VALIDATED with one caution.

Findings:

- Correctness: design solves all objectives; body audit fields preserve existing metadata and add cancel `Reason`.
- Simplicity: fixed `chore(adv)` prefix is the simplest deterministic viable approach.
- Spec-law compliance: caution â€” live `rq-cc03.1` contradicts the new contract and must be rewritten; `adv_spec show advance-delivery` parse repair is confirmed necessary for `requirements[18-22].meta.merged_from`.
- Alternatives: no significant viable alternative overlooked.

Validator recommendation: proceed. Keep the spec edit bounded to `rq-cc03.1` and malformed `meta.merged_from` fields. Ensure overlength task ID handling rejects rather than truncates.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

ADV checkpoint commits now generate release-neutral Conventional Commit-compatible subjects while preserving machine-readable audit metadata in the commit body. Acceptance review found no remaining blockers or issues after one scoped reviewer fix for explicit `changeId` fallback evidence.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Implemented checkpoint commit message generation with `chore(adv): checkpoint <task-id>` and `chore(adv): cancel checkpoint <task-id>` subjects, cancel `Reason:` body field, and deterministic invalid/overlength task ID rejection.
2. Updated ADV checkpoint documentation and `advance-delivery` spec law from old `task(...)` subjects to the new `chore(adv)` contract, including bounded metadata repair for spec parsing.
3. Verified the checkpoint contract end to end with targeted tests, spec parse, stale-subject scans, smoke checks, and acceptance review.
4. Review remediation fixed the AC5 edge case so explicit caller-provided `changeId` is used in the commit body when task lookup cannot derive one.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers, 0 issues, 0 suggestions, 0 nits.
- Tests: `pnpm --dir plugin exec vitest run src/tools/checkpoint.test.ts` passed, 20 tests; `bin/oc-test smoke` passed per reviewer report and prior execution evidence.
- Static/spec checks: worktree `SpecSchema.parse` passed for `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json` with 23 requirements; stale subject scans for `task(tk-` and `task({taskId}): {mode}` returned 0 results.
- Preview URL: not_applicable â€” agreement declares `visual_surface: false`, and implementation touched backend/tooling docs/spec/test files only; no browser-visible or visual output surface.
- Contract matrix: 27 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed, 0 violated, 0 unknown.

## Remaining Concerns

Full suite still has unrelated baseline failures reproduced on main checkout (`deploy-local.test` prompt/spec-doc drift and `temporal/messages.test` signal-surface drift). These are outside this change's accepted scope.

Subjects asserted as `chore(adv)` complete/cancel. |

| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Complete checkpoint dirty-tree commit subject matches `^chore\(adv\): checkpoint tk-[A-Za-z0-9]+$`. | pass | `checkpoint.test.ts` complete-mode assertions expect `chore(adv): checkpoint tk-AbC123` and regex `^chore\(adv\): checkpoint tk-[A-Za-z0-9]+$`; targeted test run passed 20/20. |

| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Complete and cancel checkpoint subjects are â‰¤72 chars for valid ADV task IDs, or invalid/overlength input fails deterministically before malformed commit output. | pass | `buildCommitMessage` rejects invalid task IDs and overlength subjects before commit; test `rejects task IDs that would produce overlength checkpoint subjects` passed. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Commit body includes `Task: <task-id>` and `Mode: complete|cancel`. | pass | Body construction pushes `Task: <task-id>` and `Mode: <mode>`; complete/cancel body tests passed. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Commit body includes `Change: <change-id>` when available. | pass | Reviewer fix passes `effectiveChangeId` into `buildCommitMessage`; regression `uses explicit changeId in commit body when task lookup cannot derive one` passed. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Commit body includes `Verification: <summary>` when verification supplied. | pass | Complete-mode test asserts `Verification: Tests passed`; targeted test run passed 20/20. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Cancel commit body includes `Reason: <reason>` when reason supplied; cancel reason is not embedded in subject. | pass | Cancel-mode test asserts `Reason: No longer needed` in body and subject excludes reason; targeted test run passed 20/20. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | ADV checkpoint docs/specs no longer require or cite `task(tk-xxxx): ...` generated subjects. | pass | `lgrep_search_text` in worktree found 0 results for stale generated-subject strings `task(tk-` and `task({taskId}): {mode}` after docs/spec updates. |
| AC9 | acceptance_criterion | If `advance-delivery` spec parse drift blocks spec update, malformed metadata is repaired only enough to make `adv_spec show advance-delivery` parse. | pass | Worktree spec parse succeeded with `SpecSchema.parse` for `.adv/specs/advance-delivery/spec.json` and 23 requirements. |
| AC10 | acceptance_criterion | Targeted checkpoint tests pass, and repo smoke/check path reports success. | pass | Targeted checkpoint tests passed 20/20. Reviewer-reported `bin/oc-test smoke` passed after remediation; prior execution smoke also passed. |
| C1 | constraint | Preserve checkpoint commits; do not remove or bypass checkpointing. | respected | Checkpoint commits preserved; change only alters commit-message subject/body construction and tests/specs. |
| C2 | constraint | Preserve task completion/cancellation semantics and Temporal signal behavior. | respected | Temporal/task completion signal behavior unchanged; review diff limited to message construction input and regression test. |
| C3 | constraint | Keep audit metadata machine-readable in commit body text. | respected | Audit metadata remains machine-readable body text: Change/Task/Mode/Verification/Reason. |
| C4 | constraint | Avoid repo-specific commit-policy detection or allowlist heuristics. | respected | No repo-specific commit policy detection added; subject is deterministic fixed `chore(adv)` format. |
| C5 | constraint | Keep generated subject deterministic for valid ADV task IDs. | respected | Valid ADV task IDs remain in deterministic subjects; invalid/overlength values fail before commit. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Do not drop checkpoint audit metadata. | respected | No audit metadata dropped; cancel reason moved to body and explicit changeId fallback fixed. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Do not require agents to manually squash or rewrite ADV checkpoint commits. | respected | Generated subjects now satisfy Conventional Commit shape, avoiding manual squash/rewrite for this checkpoint-policy issue. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Do not make correctness depend on heuristic detection of repository commit policy. | respected | Correctness is structural via fixed formatter, validation guards, and tests; no heuristic repo-policy detection. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Do not modify `sharperflow-security-gates` in this change. | respected | Only Advance worktree files modified; originating `sharperflow-security-gates` repo untouched. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | Do not rewrite existing branch history or existing checkpoint commits. | respected | Existing branch history/checkpoint commits were not rewritten; new behavior applies forward. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Changing task lifecycle semantics. | respected | No task lifecycle semantics changed; signal calls remain in existing flow. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Changing git checkpoint timing or when checkpoints are required. | respected | No checkpoint timing or requirement changes made; only message construction changed. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Adding repo-specific Conventional Commit policy integration. | respected | No repo-specific Conventional Commit policy integration added. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Modifying the originating repository. | respected | No files changed in originating repository; work confined to Advance worktree. |
